name: Australia
contextBlurb: Australia still has no dedicated stablecoin statute in force, but ASIC has clarified that some stablecoins can already fall within existing financial-services law. On 18 September 2025, ASIC granted class relief for intermediaries distributing a named stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer under Instrument 2025/631. On 25 September 2025, ASIC consulted on extending that relief, and on 9 December 2025 it finalised broader relief for certain stablecoins and wrapped tokens under Instrument 2025/867. Treasury's separate payment-stablecoin reform remains proposed rather than enacted, so the current regime is a mix of existing licensing law plus targeted ASIC exemptions.

legislation.title: ASIC finalises Instrument 2025/867 granting broader class relief for certain stablecoins and wrapped tokens.
legislation.summary: ASIC finalised additional relief that lets intermediaries deal in eligible stablecoins and wrapped tokens without needing separate market, clearing or financial-services licences solely because the token is itself a financial product, while keeping disclosure and record-keeping conditions in place.

legislation.title: ASIC consults on extending the stablecoin distribution exemption to a second eligible stablecoin.
legislation.summary: ASIC proposed amending Instrument 2025/631 so intermediaries could distribute a second named stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer, signalling that any expansion of relief would still be handled instrument-by-instrument under existing law.

legislation.title: ASIC grants Instrument 2025/631 for intermediaries distributing a stablecoin issued by an AFS-licensed issuer.
legislation.summary: The first stablecoin-specific ASIC relief exempts intermediaries from separate AFS, market and clearing licences when they are only dealing in a specified stablecoin issued by an AFS licensee, provided retail disclosure conditions are met.

news.headline: ASIC finalises new exemptions to support digital asset innovation
ASIC proposes extending stablecoin distribution exemption
ASIC supports innovation through exemptions for distributors of Australian stablecoin

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Australia does not yet have a dedicated stablecoin act in force. Instead, ASIC is using existing financial-services law plus targeted class relief: Instrument 2025/631 (18 September 2025) for distribution of a named stablecoin issued by an AFS licensee, a consultation on extending that relief (25 September 2025), and broader final relief for certain stablecoins and wrapped tokens under Instrument 2025/867 (9 December 2025). Treasury's payment-stablecoin reform remains proposed, so the regime is still developing.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Stablecoins are classified by function under the Corporations Act. Where a token enables payments, it will often be a non-cash payment (NCP) facility, making it a financial product that triggers AFSL and disclosure obligations. regulators.name: Australian Securities and Investments Commission
regulators.role: ASIC is the primary regulator for stablecoins, overseeing licensing of issuers, granting temporary exemptions for distributors, and enforcing compliance with financial services laws.

regulators.name: Treasury
regulators.role: Treasury is responsible for developing and proposing legislation to regulate digital asset platforms, stablecoins, and payment services, including the exposure draft legislation released in 2025.
